hints.title: Nudge
hints.content: The login form is secure, but there's another entry point. Check the cookies — is there a 'remember me' token being sent?

hints.title: Direction
hints.content: The 'remember_token' cookie is used in: SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE token='COOKIE_VALUE'. If you set this cookie to a SQL injection payload, you can bypass the session lookup.

hints.title: Approach
hints.content: Set the remember_token cookie to: ' OR 1=1 LIMIT 1-- to match the first session in the database. Or target admin specifically: ' OR user_id=1--

hints.title: Near-solution
hints.content: Use browser devtools or curl to set the cookie: remember_token=' OR user_id=(SELECT id FROM users WHERE username='admin')-- Then visit the main page — you should be authenticated as admin.

hints.title: Solution
hints.content: 1) Open browser devtools > Application > Cookies. 2) Set cookie 'remember_token' to: ' OR user_id=1-- 3) Refresh the page. The SQL becomes: SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE token='' OR user_id=1--' which finds admin's session. 4) You're now logged in as admin and can see the flag in the inbox.
